title: Compositions and methods for treating disorders of genomic imprinting
abstract: Disclosed herein are compositions, kits, and methods for treating a disorder of genomic imprinting in a subject. The method may include modifying a zinc-finger protein 274 (ZNF274) binding site on maternal chromosome 15 at position 15q11-q13 of the subject, such that the binding of a ZNF274 protein to the ZNF274 binding site is reduced relative to a control. The ZNF274 binding site comprises a polynucleotide having at least 90% sequence identify to SEQ ID NO: 1 or SEQ ID NO: 42. Further provided are DNA targeting systems that bind to a ZNF274 binding site or to a gene encoding a ZNF274 protein.
claims: Claims CLAIMS  We claim: 1. A guide RNA (gRNA) molecule comprising a polynucleotides sequence corresponding to SEQ ID NO: 13, SEQ ID NO: 14, SEQ ID NO: 43, SEQ ID NO: 44, SEQ ID NO: 45, SEQ ID NO: 46, SEQ ID NO: 47, SEQ ID NO: 48 or SEQ ID NO: 48. 2. A DNA targeting system that binds to a ZNF274 binding site, the DNA targeting system comprising at least one gRNA that binds and targets a polynucleotide sequence comprising a nucleotide sequence corresponding to at least one of SEQ ID NO: 1 , SEQ ID NO: 42, SEQ ID NO: 43, SEQ ID NO: 44, SEQ ID NO: 45, SEQ ID NO: 46, or variant thereof. 3. The DNA targeting system of claim 2, wherein the at least one gRNA comprises a polynucleotide sequence corresponding to at least one of SEQ ID NO: 43, SEQ ID NO: 44, SEQ ID NO: 45, SEQ ID NO: 46, or variant thereof. 4. A DNA targeting system that binds to a gene encoding a ZNF274 protein, the DNA targeting system comprising at least one gRNA that binds and targets a polynucleotide sequence comprising a nucleotide sequence corresponding to at least one of SEQ ID NO: 13, SEQ ID NO: 14, SEQ ID NO: 16, SEQ ID NO: 17, SEQ ID NO: 47, SEQ ID NO: 48, or variant thereof. 5. The DNA targeting system of claim 4, wherein the at least one gRNA comprises a polynucleotide sequence corresponding to at least one of SEQ ID NO: 13, SEQ ID NO: 14, SEQ ID NO: 47, SEQ ID NO: 48, or variant thereof. 6. The DNA targeting system of any one of claims 2-5, further comprising a Clustered Regularly Interspaced Short Palindromic Repeats associated (Cas) protein. 7. The DNA targeting system of claim 6, wherein the Cas protein comprises a Streptococcus pyogenes Cas9 molecule, or variant thereof. 8. The DNA targeting system of claim 7, wherein the Cas protein comprises a VQR variant of the S. pyogenes Cas9 molecule. 9. The DNA targeting system of claim 6, wherein the Cas protein comprises a Cas9 that recognizes a Protospacer Adjacent Motif (PAM) of NGG (SEQ ID NO: 2), NGA (SEQ ID NO: 3), NGAN (SEQ ID NO: 49) or NGNG (SEQ ID NO: 50). 10. An isolated polynucleotide sequence comprising the gRNA molecule of claim 1. 11. An isolated polynucleotide sequence encoding the DNA targeting system of any one of claims 2-9. 12. A vector comprising the isolated polynucleotide sequence of claim 10 or 11. 13. A vector encoding the gRNA molecule of claim 1 and a Clustered Regularly Interspaced Short Palindromic Repeats associated (Cas) protein. 14. The vector of claim 13, wherein the Cas protein comprises a Streptococcus pyogenes Cas9 molecule, or variant thereof. 15. The vector of claim 14, wherein the Cas protein comprises a VQR variant of the S. pyogenes Cas9 molecule. 16. A cell comprising the gRNA of claim 1 , the DNA targeting system of any one of claims 2-9, the isolated polynucleotide sequence of claim 10 or 11 , or the vector of any one of claims 12-15, or a combination thereof. 17. The cell of claim 16, wherein the cell is an Induced Pluripotent Stem Cell (iPSC) from a Prader-Will syndrome (PWS) patient. 18. The cell of claim 17, wherein the iPSC is a PWS 1-7 large deletion cell line or UPD 1-2 cell line. 19. A kit comprising the gRNA of claim 1 , the DNA targeting system of any one of claims 2-9, the isolated polynucleotide sequence of claim 10 or 11 , or the vector of any one of claims 12-15, or the cell of any one of claims 16-18, or a combination thereof. 20. A pharmaceutical composition comprising the gRNA of claim 1 , the DNA targeting system of any one of claims 2-9, the isolated polynucleotide sequence of claim 10 or 11 , or the vector of any one of claims 12-15, or the cell of any one of claims 16-18, or a combination thereof. 21. A method for treating a disorder of genomic imprinting in a subject, the method comprising: modifying a zinc-finger protein 274 (ZNF274) binding site on maternal chromosome 15 at position 15q11-q13 of the subject, such that the binding of a ZNF274 protein to the ZNF274 binding site is reduced relative to a control, wherein the ZNF274 binding site comprises a polynucleotide having at least 90% sequence identity to SEQ ID NO: 1 or SEQ ID NO: 42. 22. The method of claim 21 , wherein binding of the ZNF274 protein to the ZNF274 binding site is reduced by at least 90% relative to a control. 23. The method of claim 21 , wherein binding of the ZNF274 protein to the ZNF274 binding site is eliminated. 24. The method of any one of claims 21-23, wherein the maternal chromosome 15 at position 15q11-q13 of the subject is silenced prior to modification of the ZNF274 binding site. 25. The method of any one of claims 21-24, wherein the disorder comprises Prader- Will syndrome (PWS). 26. The method of any one of claims 21-25, wherein the ZNF274 binding site is modified by fully deleting the ZNF274 binding site, partially deleting the ZNF274 binding site, mutating one or more nucleotides of the ZNF274 binding site, cutting the ZNF274 binding site at one or more nucleotide positions, or a combination thereof. 27. The method of any one of claims 21-26, wherein the ZNF274 binding site is modified by administering to the subject or a cell of the subject a DNA targeting system that binds to the ZNF274 binding site, wherein the DNA targeting system comprises at least one gRNA that binds and targets a polynucleotide sequence corresponding to SEQ ID NO: 1, SEQ ID NO: 42, or variant thereof. 28. The method of any one of claims 21-26, wherein the ZNF274 binding site is modified by administering an isolated polynucleotide encoding a DNA targeting system that binds to the ZNF274 binding site, the DNA targeting system comprising at least one gRNA that binds and targets a polynucleotide sequence corresponding to SEQ ID NO: 1, SEQ ID NO: 42, or variant thereof. 29. The method of claim 27 or 28, wherein the DNA targeting system comprises at least one gRNA that binds and targets a polynucleotide sequence comprising a nucleotide sequence corresponding to at least one of SEQ ID NO: 1, SEQ ID NO: 42, SEQ ID NO: 43, SEQ ID NO: 44, SEQ ID NO: 45, SEQ ID NO: 46, or variant thereof. 30. The method of any one claims 27-29, wherein the at least one gRNA comprises a polynucleotide sequence corresponding to at least one of SEQ ID NO: 43, SEQ ID NO: 44, SEQ ID NO: 45, SEQ ID NO: 46, or variant thereof. 31. The method of any one claims 27-30, wherein the DNA targeting system further comprises a Clustered Regularly Interspaced Short Palindromic Repeats associated (Cas) protein. 32. A method for treating a disorder of genomic imprinting in a subject, the method comprising: administering to the subject a pharmaceutically effective amount of an agent that reduces the interaction of a ZNF274 protein with a ZNF274 binding site on maternal chromosome 15 at position 15q11-q13 of the subject relative to a control, wherein the ZNF274 binding site comprises a polynucleotide having at least 90% sequence identity to SEQ ID NO: 1 or SEQ ID NO: 42. 33. The method of claim 32, wherein the agent comprises a sequence-specific nuclease, or a polynucleotide sequence encoding a sequence-specific nuclease. 34. The method of claim 33, wherein the sequence-specific nuclease comprises a zinc finger nuclease, a TAL effector nuclease, or a CRISPR/Cas9 DNA targeting system. 35. The method of claim 34, wherein the CRISPR/Cas9 DNA targeting system binds to the ZNF274 binding site and comprises at least one gRNA that binds and targets a polynucleotide sequence comprising a nucleotide sequence corresponding to at least one of SEQ ID NO: 1 , SEQ ID NO: 42, SEQ ID NO: 43, SEQ ID NO: 44, SEQ ID NO: 45, SEQ ID NO: 46, or variant thereof. 36. The method of claim 34 or 35, wherein the at least one gRNA comprises a polynucleotide sequence corresponding to at least one of SEQ ID NO: 43, SEQ ID NO: 44, SEQ ID NO: 45, SEQ ID NO: 46, or variant thereof. 37. The method of any one of claims 34-36, wherein the CRISPR/Cas9 DNA targeting system further comprises a Clustered Regularly Interspaced Short Palindromic Repeats associated (Cas) protein. 38. The method of claim 37, wherein the Cas protein comprises a Cas9. 39. The method of claim 37 or 38, wherein the Cas protein comprises a Streptococcus pyogenes Cas9 molecule, or variant thereof. 40. The method of claim 39, wherein the Cas protein comprises a VQR variant of the S. pyogenes Cas9 molecule. 41. The method of any one of claims 21-40 where the expression of at least one gene within 15q11-q13 is increased. 42. The method of any one of claims 21-40, where the expression of at least one gene within the Prader-Will Syndrome critical region (PWSCR) of 15q11-q13 is increased. 43. The method of any one of claims 21-40, wherein the expression of at least one RNA transcript selected from the genome coordinates hg19 chr15:25,012,961-25,685,253 or chr15:23,695,603-25,026,558 is increased. 44. The method of any one of claims 21-40, wherein the expression of at least one RNA transcript selected from SNORD116, IPW, SNORD115, SNHG14, UBE3A-ATS, or a combination thereof, is increased. 45. The method of any one of claims 21-40, wherein the expression of at least one of SNRPN exon 2, SNRPN exon 3, SNRPN exon 4, UBE3A, MAGEL2, MKRN3, SNRPN exon U4, NDN, or a combination thereof, is increased. 46. The method of any one of claims 21-40, wherein the initiation of transcription from the SNRPN U1A promoter, the SNRPN U1 B promoter, or a combination thereof, is increased. 47. The method of any one of claims 21-40, wherein the binding of H3K9me3 is reduced. 48. A formulation for treating a disorder of genomic imprinting in a subject, the formulation comprising an agent that reduces relative to a control the binding of a ZNF274 protein to a ZNF274 binding site on a maternal nucleotide sequence, the ZNF274 binding site comprising a polynucleotide having at least 90% sequence identity to SEQ ID NO: 1 or SEQ ID NO: 42. 49. The formulation of claim 48, wherein the disorder is Prader-Will syndrome (PWS). 50. The formulation of claim 48, where the agent activates expression of at least one gene within 15q11-q13. 51. The formulation of claim 48, where the agent activates expression of at least one gene within the Prader-Will Syndrome critical region (PWSCR) of 15q11-q13. 52. The formulation of claim 45, wherein the agent activates the expression of at least one RNA transcript selected from the genome coordinates hg19 chrl 5:25,012,961- 25,685,253 or chrl 5:23,695,603-25,026,558 is increased. 53. The formulation of claim 48, wherein the agent activates expression of at least one RNA transcript selected from SNORD116, IPW, SNORD115, SNHG14, UBE3A-ATS, or a combination thereof. 54. The formulation of claim 48, wherein the agent activates expression of at least one of SNRPN exon 2, SNRPN exon 3, SNRPN exon 4, UBE3A, MAGEL2, MKRN3, SNRPN exon U4, NDN, or a combination thereof. 55. The formulation of claim 48, wherein the agent activates the initiation of transcription from the SNRPN U1 A promoter, the SNRPN U1 B promoter, or a combination thereof. 56. The formulation of claim 48, wherein the agent reduces the binding of H3K9me3. 57. The method of any one of claims 21-47 or the formulation of any one of claims 48-56 wherein the control comprises a ZNF274 binding site that has not been modified.
